strengths: Built and shipped production AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq, including MCP code generators that support agentic workflows — directly aligning with the need for tool-augmented, stateful agents beyond prompt chaining.

Demonstrated deep full-stack and distributed-systems expertise across high-throughput environments (e.g., millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, real-time Geo-Data platform with Kubernetes), matching required backend and event-streaming proficiency.

Proven track record of engineering leadership through open-source stewardship (GISCollective), team mentoring (Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos), and hybrid delivery models — showing ability to scale technical culture and govern external contributions.
concerns: No explicit mention of fintech infrastructure experience (credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance) — a meaningful advantage in the JD, though not a hard requirement.
Current role at re:cinq is listed as starting April 2025 — which is in the future — raising a minor red flag about resume accuracy or timing clarity.
Limited visibility into direct experience with LLM evaluation pipelines, rate-limiting strategies, or multi-jurisdictional auditability frameworks — key technical depth areas in the JD.
response.justification: Bogdan brings rare, verifiable production experience building agentic AI infrastructure and high-scale distributed systems — directly matching the core technical demands of the role — while also demonstrating consistent leadership in cultivating engineering discipline, mentoring teams, and governing open-source and external contributions, making him a compelling fit despite limited fintech domain exposure.
